title: Baccharis yungensis
taxonomy: Baccharis yungensis is classified within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Baccharis, which is a group of flowering plants native to South America. The species Baccharis yungensis is further classified as a member of the subfamily Asteroideae. Within the genus Baccharis, Baccharis yungensis is considered to be part of the tribe Baccharideae. Its classification is also supported by phylogenetic analysis, which places it within the clade of South American Baccharis species.

intro: Baccharis yungensis is a plant species of the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the shrub or tree baccharis, native to South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been reported to help alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Baccharis yungensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Baccharis yungensis is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and also used as a tincture or ointment to treat various health conditions.
